title: euonymus fortunei f. angustifolius
intro: Euonymus fortunei f. angustifolius, also known as the Chinese spindle or narrow-leaved fortune's spindletree, is a shrub or small tree native to East Asia, characterized by its dark green, narrow leaves and small red berries. The plant is used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral effects. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, alleviating pain, and promoting blood circulation, making it a popular ingredient in herbal remedies. In herbal preparations, the leaves and stems of the plant are typically dried and used in teas, infusions, and decoctions to harness its medicinal properties.
